title: C++ Cheat Sheet
description: Essential C++ commands and syntax for software engineers.

commands.description: Compile a C++ source file into an executable.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to compile a C++ program from source code.
Use after compiling your C++ program to execute it.

commands.description: Basic structure of a C++ program.
commands.scenario: Use as a starting point for any C++ application.

commands.description: Print a message to the console.
commands.scenario: Use to output text to the console in a C++ program.
Use when you need a dynamic array to store integers.

commands.description: Sort a vector in ascending order.
commands.scenario: Use to sort elements in a vector.

commands.description: Declare and initialize a string variable.
commands.scenario: Use to store and manipulate text in C++.

commands.command: for (int i = 0; i < 10; ++i) { std::cout << i << std::endl; }
commands.description: Loop from 0 to 9 and print each number.
commands.scenario: Use to iterate over a range of numbers.

commands.description: Declare a map with string keys and integer values.
commands.scenario: Use to associate keys with values for quick lookup.

commands.description: Insert a key-value pair into a map.
commands.scenario: Use to add or update entries in a map.
Use to read data from a file.

commands.description: Read a line from a file into a string.
commands.scenario: Use to read text line-by-line from a file.

commands.command: try { /* code */ } catch (const std::exception& e) { std::cerr << e.what() << std::endl; }
commands.scenario: Use to handle exceptions and errors gracefully.
